text: Two 10mm ports are placed in the right and left upper quadrants, with two additional 5mm ports at the umbilicus level. The surgeon performs sharp dissection to reduce the hernia contents. A mesh is inserted through a 10mm port using a tool that tightly rolls the mesh. A suture passer is introduced into the defect's center, and the balloon caster in the mesh's center is pulled to the exterior. The balloon is inflated with air using a syringe system, spreading the mesh for easy positioning without orientation sutures.

The surgeon places circumferential tacks around the balloon using absorbo tucks. After completing the full circumferential layer of tacks, the balloon is deflated and extracted through the ten millimeter port.

Pressure is applied from the exterior to counteract the attackers while forming the inner layer of the mesh, which is hydrophilic and bowel-friendly. After removing the balloon, a second layer of circumferential non-absorbable tacks is applied to securely fix the mesh to the abdominal wall.
The final appearance of the mesh is shown, securely positioned and covering all hernia defects. Diagrams and illustrations of the mesh components and their operation are displayed.
